content.question: Will expanding school choice or selective-entry mechanisms in Hungary risk replicating Finland's documented pattern of performance-based sorting and school fragmentation, potentially increasing segregation of SNI-identified and disadvantaged students?
content.reduction_path: Model choice-system design options (e.g., open-choice vs. preference-weighted vs. centralized assignment) using Hungarian school-location and demographics data; conduct pilot choice expansion in selected urban areas with detailed tracking of segregation indices and SNI-student distribution across schools pre- and post-choice implementation; document municipal policies for managing cream-skimming or adverse selection.
